invariants_checked: MEM-0073 external sources as research inputs
MEM-0077 Codexter invocation is normal Codex plus installed skills

summary: The implementation lands the requested normal-Codex invocation seam without turning Codexter into a standalone runner: WORKFLOW.md defines policy, the helper normalizes one filesystem ticket and validates compute/route/proof shape, the skill package tells Codex how to use the route, and proof artifacts exercise ready, approval-blocked, future-compute-blocked, and proof-packet paths.

rubric_sections.name: code-quality
findings: The helper is intentionally small, pure Python, and diagnostic-only; it validates contracts, returns explicit blockers, writes proof JSON, and does not launch Codex or own orchestration.
The constrained frontmatter parser is a deliberate v1 tradeoff that is covered for the policy shapes used here and fails loudly on unsupported structures.
rubric_sections.next_action: Keep future board/cloud adapter logic out of this helper unless a later ticket introduces a real adapter boundary.

rubric_sections.name: integration-readiness
findings: The ticket metadata validator, ticket docs, skill package, root workflow file, feature registry, README, ARCHITECTURE, HISTORY, MEMORY, and AGENTS surfaces now agree on the same boundary.
Unsupported compute targets are visible and blocked, including the future Symphony target, so cloud/background execution does not look accidentally available.
rubric_sections.next_action: Advance this ticket to closeout; create separate follow-ups for any real Symphony/Linear/Notion adapter or parallel dispatcher.

rubric_sections.name: evidence-quality
findings: Unit tests cover workflow parsing, planning route, approval-gated build blocking, future compute blocking, proof writing, proof path containment, and ticket metadata validation.
Attached dry-run JSON artifacts prove the allowed planning route, approval-blocked build route, future Symphony-compute blocker, and parseable proof-packet shape.
rubric_sections.next_action: Keep the attached artifacts linked from the ticket so future Symphony work can reuse the same request/result examples.

verified_commands: python3 -m py_compile bin/codexter_invocation.py bin/test_codexter_invocation.py tickets/scripts/check_ticket_metadata.py bin/test_ticket_metadata.py
next_action: Run close-ticket/archive flow for TASK-0107 when ready; future remote board, polling, and cloud compute work should be separate tickets.
